programmes.show_description: Sam Fender: Brian and Mark visit Mark's own British Grove recording studio to meet fellow Geordie Sam Fender, who discusses his musical journey so far. (S1, ep 2)
The greatest rock star of the last 30 years, a man of the people, "the King of Knebworth," gives 160,000 fans hope and joy through his music.
Knebworth 22 tells the story behind Liam's epic return to Knebworth as a solo artist, which saw 170,000 fans attend two sold-out shows.

programmes.show_name: Inside Art: Eileen Agar At Whitechapel Gallery
programmes.show_description: Kate Bryan steps into the beguiling world of Eileen Agar, exploring her exhibition entitled Angel of Anarchy at the historic Whitechapel Gallery.
...Orchestra's 100 Years of Big Bands. Join the Len Phillips Swing Orchestra - one of Britain's most popular big bands - to celebrate a century of swing. (1 of 2)
...Orchestra's 100 Years of Big Bands. Join the Len Phillips Swing Orchestra - one of Britain's most popular big bands - to celebrate a century of swing. (2 of 2)
Back For Christmas: A philandering physician with an obsession for orchids cultivates a deadly plot to see off his wife. Siân Phillips and Richard Johnson star. (S3, ep 1)
Picture Of A Place: Hazel is an elderly widow who lives in a farmhouse full of antiques. When a conman arrives he dupes her into selling him a picture at a knock-down price. (S3, ep 2)
Proof Of Guilt: George is denying murder, even though he was in a locked room with the victim when he was shot. Inspector Walters just can't find any evidence against him. (S3, ep 3)
Vengeance Is Mine Inc: Mrs Wilbur gets more than she bargained for when she calls upon the services of Tom and George, two actors from New York who are hell bent on revenge. (S3, ep 4)
The Sign of Satan: When a studio wants Karl for their next horror film, he reveals his audition film was actually footage of a real black mass with devil worshipers. (S2, ep 27)
Who Needs An Enemy?: A frightened embezzler stages his own suicide with the help of his double-crossing girlfriend. (S2, ep 28)
The working-class Smiths change their initially sunny views on World War I after the three boys of the family witness the harsh reality of trench warfare.

programmes.show_name: California Dreamin': The Songs Of The Mamas & The Papas
programmes.show_description: Interviews and performance footage tells the remarkable story of 1960s Californian pop group The Mamas and the Papas.

programmes.show_name: Agatha Christie's The Pale Pale Horse
programmes.show_description: A man is determined to find out why he's on a list of names found in the shoe of a dead woman and a trio of witches is believed to be responsible for a series of deaths.
Live in Vegas: The Bee Gees perform their greatest hits from the 60s, 70s, 80s and 90s, with guests including Celine Dion. From the MGM in Las Vegas in 1997.

programmes.show_name: The Good, The Bad And The Ugly
programmes.show_description: A bounty-hunting scam joins two men in an uneasy alliance against a third in a race to find a fortune in gold buried in a remote cemetery. Clint Eastwood's sprawling Western epic. (1966)

programmes.show_name: The Songs Of Marc Bolan & T
programmes.show_description: Rex: AngelHeaded Hipster. Documentary celebrating T. Rex frontman Marc Bolan, with a behind-the-scenes look at the album of the same name. Features interviews with music icons.
ABC: Accompanied by the Southbank Sinfonia at the London Palladium, pop band ABC perform and discuss The Lexicon of Love, one of the 1980s' most successful debut albums. (S1, ep 3)
Leading music critics and DJs discuss the life, music, legacy and larger-than-life stage characters of the glam rock legend.
Face Value: Phil Collins reveals how he turned personal turmoil into an acclaimed debut solo album, which features arguably his most enduring hit, In The Air Tonight. (S1, ep 14)
A reimagined account of the early life of Maria Anna 'Nannerl' Mozart, five years older than Wolfgang and a musical prodigy in her own right.
A Girl Can't Always Have Everything: Struggling actresses Suzy and Pat meet Herbert, a wealthy widower. Seductive Suzy enjoys his money, but things soon don't look so rosy. (S3, ep 5)
Parson's Pleasure: Cyril Boggis makes a profitable living as a rogue antiques dealer. But when he buys Farmer Rummins' valuable furniture, his luck may be about to run out. (S3, ep 6)
The Stinker: When millionaire Jack runs into Harold, a boy he bullied at school, he gives him a job. But Harold soon suspects his wife of having an affair with his new boss. (S3, ep 7)
I'll Be Seeing You: When Roland leaves his cruel, millionairess wife Vivien for a fresh start with his mistress Anna, the snubbed spouse plans to make his life a living hell. (S3, ep 8)
The Party: When disgruntled factory manager Harry is left feeling undervalued by his colleagues, he decides to take revenge. (S3, ep 9)
Bed of Roses: A married man finds his beautiful mistress murdered and flees without reporting the crime and soon becomes the victim of a blackmailer. (S2, ep 29)
The Second Verdict: An ethical lawyer becomes very disturbed about what to do when the client he just got acquitted of murder brags that he committed the crime. (S2, ep 30)
Saturn Devouring His Son: Chart the darkness, fear and haunting imagination that fuelled Francisco Goya's work, including the turbulent times that guided each brutal stroke. (S1, ep 3)
The Garden of Earthly Delights: Explore the surreal landscapes, fantastical figures and moral allegories that made Hieronymus Bosch's triptych stand out in its era. (S1, ep 4)
An investigator from the War Crimes Commission travels to Connecticut to find an infamous Nazi.

programmes.show_name: Simon & Garfunkel: Old Friends Live on Stage
programmes.show_description: The Duo perform their third live album with performances taken from shows at Madison Square Garden and New Jersey in December 2003.
Join Paul Simon inside the studio as he makes his new album Seven Psalms, while looking back on his six-decade career's countless musical peaks. (1 of 2)
The Final Reunion. Performed live in front of 15,000 fans at London's O2 Arena, Cliff and The Shadows' 50th anniversary show recreates some of their biggest hits.
Prepare to be blown away by performances from Opry member Carly Pearce, Ashley McBryde, Martina McBride, Crystal Gayle, Emmy Russell and Rhonda Vincent.
Coverage of the iconic Isle of Wight festival, one of the UK's biggest and most legendary highlights of the summer. Featuring James.
